name: Appen Platform API
description:
# Introduction
Hello, and welcome to Appen, the leading data annotation platform for Machine Learning. Our API enables developers to build applications that can programmatically create, edit, and launch Appen jobs, download results, and more! 

Appen uses a RESTful API that accepts data requests as URL-encoded key-value pairs. Responses are returned in JSON and authentication is key-based.

Before integrating with the API, we highly recommend you first build and run a job using our [graphical user interface](https://client.appen.com/sessions/new). Once you have run a job successfully and are satisfied with the results, you can automate the process using the Appen API.

# Authentication
You will need to supply your Appen API key to access the API. To find your API key:
1. Click the circular account icon located in the top right corner of any page and then select the Profile option from the dropdown.
![Appen Authentication](/assets/img/authentication1.png "Appen Authentication")
2. Click the API tab and you can use Generate New to create an API key. Note that creating a new API key will invalidate any previous API keys for this user so remember to update any code using the previous key.
![Appen API](/assets/img/authentication2.png "Appen API key")

For requests listed below, the API Key will be listed as `{api_key}`. 

The API key needs to be included in the header for every request: 
```
  headers: { 

   "AUTHORIZATION" => "Token token={api_key}" 

  } 
```

You may also see other variables, such as `{job_id}` for operations pertaining to a specific job, or `{team_id}` when completing operations relevant to an entire team (like listing all available jobs). Job ID's can be determined from the job listing page or the URL. A team id is visible in the URL of the Teams tab in the Account page. 
# Resources
There are three primary data objects on Appen that correspond to resources on the API:
## Jobs
A **Job** is an interface that connects your data to an online workforce.
  Each job on the Appen platform has data, instructions, customizable questions for your use case
  (written in **CML** [](https://success.appen.com/hc/en-us/articles/202817989-CML-Custom-Markup-Language-Overview)),
  [test questions](https://success.appen.com/hc/en-us/articles/202702985-How-to-Create-Test-Questions),
  and is worked on by *Contributors*. Contributors submit *Judgments* on the rows of data via a worker interface. 
    * All jobs in a single account can be found [on your jobs page](https://client.appen.com/jobs) and will be
    identified by a unique numeric id.
## Units (also known as 'Rows')
A **Unit** of data is uploaded to the job from your source data file via API or GUI. 
## Judgments 
A **Judgment** is the set of responses submitted by a contributor on a particular unit of data. It is
  recommended to collect multiple judgments and compare them to one another or aggregate to the top response. For
  each job, you can specify the number of judgments you would like each row to receive.
# Responses and Messaging
Each request to the Appen API returns an HTTP status code response and one or both of the following:
* An application response message (in JSON) 
* A JSON representation of the object (job, unit, or judgments) requested
See the below sections of different API Requests, Responses and Messaging for more information:
1. [Job Create/Update](/#tag/Job-CreateUpdate)
2. [Manage Job Data](/#tag/Manage-Job-Data)
3. [Job Status](/#tag/Job-Status)
4. [Monitor Contributors](/#tag/Monitor-Contributors)
5. [Manage Job Settings](/#tag/Manage-Job-Settings)
6. [Job Results](/#tag/Job-Results)
7. [Account Info](/#tag/Account-Info)
8. [Project Flow](/#tag/Project-Flow)
